data.title: Planet Evening News | December 13
data.description: 1. ADNOC, the UAE's largest fuel retailer, will begin accepting stablecoin payments; 2. Coinbase will suspend order book trading for some ANKR, AXS, CGLD, and LRC trading pairs; 3. A US Bitcoin company added 613 BTC this week, bringing its total holdings to $444 million; 4. 0G Foundation: Contract attack resulted in the theft of 520,000 0G; 5. Grayscale transferred approximately 14,684 ETH and 252 BTC, worth over $70 million, to Coinbase Prime; 6. Solana Mobile: Partnering with a chipmaker, plans to integrate hardware into over 2 billion Android phones; 7. MERL briefly broke through 0.44 USDT, now ranking among the top 100 in market capitalization on CoinMarketCap; 8. Data: Pump Fun trading volume has declined for four consecutive months; 9. Crypto derivatives exchange Bitnomial received CFTC approval to launch a prediction market.

data.title: Kenya’s DCI launches a special unit to fight growing ‘crypto’ fraud
data.description:
Kenya’s Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI) has announced the establishment of a specialised unit to investigate crypto fraud. The decision was made due to increased criminal activity and mounting losses of up to KES5. 6 billion ($43. 3 million) among local investors in the past year. According to the DCI, the new unit will focus on crypto […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/kenyas-dci-special-unit-fight-crypto-fraud/

data.title: ADNOC to Accept Stablecoin Payments at Fuel Stations in Three Countries
data.description: According to Foresight News, the Abu Dhabi National Oil Company (ADNOC), the largest fuel retailer in the United Arab Emirates, will begin accepting stablecoin payments at 980 fuel stations across three countries. This move marks a significant step in integrating digital currency into everyday transactions within the region.

data.title: ADNOC, the UAE's largest fuel retailer, will begin accepting stablecoin payments.
data.description: ADNOC Distribution announced a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) with Al Maryah Community Bank to accept AE Coin stablecoin payments across its retail network of nearly 980 service points in the UAE, Saudi Arabia, and Egypt. Customers can use AE Coin at gas pumps, Oasis by ADNOC convenience stores, and car washes via Al Maryah Community Bank's AEC Wallet. AE Coin is the UAE's first central bank-licensed stablecoin, pegged 1:1 to the UAE Dirham. Eng. Bader Saeed Al Lamki, CEO of ADNOC Distribution, stated that this move is a significant step for the company in bringing secure, regulated digital payments to everyday retail. ADNOC Distribution operates 562 sites in the UAE, 172 sites in Saudi Arabia, and 243 sites in Egypt.

data.title: Ribbon Finance hacked for $2.7 million
data.description:
A sophisticated attack on Aevo-rebrand Ribbon Finance drained $2. 7 million from its old contract and moved to fifteen separate wallet addresses, some of which have already been consolidated into larger accounts. According to several blockchain investigators on social platform X, the attack occurred just six days after the platform upgraded its oracle infrastructure and option […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/ribbon-finance-hacked-for-2-7-million/

data.title: Ripple Breaks Into Europe’s Banking Sector With AMINA Partnership
data.description:
Ripple has secured a major milestone in its global expansion strategy, signing AMINA Bank as its first European banking client for Ripple Payments, the company’s blockchain-based cross-border settlement platform. The partnership marks a significant step in Ripple’s push to deepen adoption of its payments infrastructure across regulated financial institutions.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/ripple-breaks-into-europes-banking-sector-with-amina-partnership/
